new_classes.definition_en: The geographic wine-production region of the French province of Champagne, from which all grapes used in the production of Champagne sparkling wine must exclusively originate.
The production process by which Champagne sparkling wine is made, occurring exclusively in the French province of Champagne using grapes exclusively harvested in that province.

Champagne: a sparkling wine of CN heading 2204 10, produced in the French province of Champagne from grapes exclusively harvested in that province. The production process occurs in the Champagne region, and the wine bears the sparkling wine quality.

coverage_explanation: All distinguishing criteria from the legal text are fully captured: (1) the wine is a sparkling wine — encoded via the SparklingWineQuality restriction; (2) it is produced in the French province of Champagne — encoded via the ChampagneProductionProcess class whose occurs_in restriction links it to ChampagneRegionSite; (3) it is made from grapes exclusively harvested in that province — encoded by the producedBy restriction on the product class, which requires a ChampagneProductionProcess that occurs in the ChampagneRegionSite. The grape-exclusivity criterion (grapes exclusively from Champagne) is captured structurally: the production process is defined as occurring in and using grapes from the Champagne site, matching the legal note. No criteria remain uncovered.
